{{Infobox book
| name = The Crimson Vault
| slug = tg-02-crimson-vault
| precedes = [[City of Light (novel)|City of Light]]
| follows = [[House of Blades (novel)|House of Blades]]
| cover = [[File:Cover-tg-02-crimson-vault.jpg|250px]]
| released = 2013
| isbn = 9780989671798
| publisher = Hidden Gnome Publishing
| pages = 384
}}

== Plot summary ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The story begins with the warrior [[Indirial]] protecting a young boy named [[Simon]] from [[Enosh Travelers]] in the [[Latari Forest]], an act that sets [[Simon]] on a path through the trials of [[Valinhall]]. While [[Simon]] struggles with his past and encounters the resentful [[Erastes]], [[Kai]] battles [[obsidian wolves]] in the [[Badari Desert]] before retrieving the legendary [[sword]] [[Mithra]]. Using this blade, the Eldest{{!}}Eldest Nye breaks a seal at the Ragnarus tree{{!}}Ragnarus Tree, releasing the long-imprisoned [[Valinhall Incarnation]], [[Valin]]. This release sends shockwaves through the realm, prompting various leaders to react.

In [[Damasca]], political instability rises as [[Leah]], a [[Damascan]] heiress acting as a spy, observes her brother [[Talos]]'s failed assassination attempt on King Zakareth VI{{!}}King Zakareth. As the [[Grandmasters of Enosh]], led by [[Naraka]], plan a preemptive strike against [[Damasca]] to capitalize on the chaos of [[Valin]]'s escape, [[Leah]] travels to [[Helgard]] to warn her father. Meanwhile, [[Alin]], a powerful figure celebrating his victory over [[Overlord Malachi]], finds himself caught in the escalating conflict. The tension peaks when [[Simon]] and [[Kai]] are forced to defend against assassins sent by [[Naraka]], eventually joining forces to hunt the escaped [[Incarnation]].

The conflict intensifies as [[Valin]] begins a violent march toward the capital, [[Cana]]. During this chaos, [[Alin]] and his companions attempt to destroy the Overlords' [[Trees]] but are ambushed by mysterious travelers. [[Valin]]’s presence begins to physically transform the landscape, leading to a massive confrontation where King Zakareth VI{{!}}King Zakareth intervenes with his army. Amidst these battles, [[Simon]] discovers the immense and draining power of a modified [[Ragnarus mask]], while [[Leah]] is named the royal successor to the [[Damascan]] throne. 

As the war reaches a fever pitch, [[Talos]] proposes a desperate alliance with the [[Grandmasters]] to strike at the [[Hanging Tree]]. During a chaotic battlefield engagement involving [[Avernus Travelers]] and massive creatures, [[Talos]] betrays [[Kai]], but the focus shifts when [[Simon]], utilizing the power of his The mask{{!}}mask, engages [[Valin]] in a high-speed duel. With [[Leah]]'s assistance to bind Incarnation{{!}}the Incarnation, [[Simon]] delivers a fatal blow to [[Valin]] using [[Talos's sword]]. However, the victory is hollow; King Zakareth VI{{!}}King Zakareth is killed during the ensuing turmoil in the [[Crimson Vault]], and [[Alin]] escapes with a [[Hanging Tree seed]].

The aftermath is devastating. [[Grandmaster Naraka]] pursues her own agenda involving the seed, while [[Alin]] returns to find his home in [[Myria]] destroyed and his sisters dead or under attack. In a surge of immense power, [[Alin]] kills an [[Incarnation]] but is himself transformed into one. The novel concludes with a broken [[Simon]], now in [[Abdera]], receiving orders from the transformed [[Alin]] to protect [[Ilana]]. As [[Simon]] joins forces with [[Leah]], [[Indirial]], and [[Kai]] to form a resistance against the roaming [[Incarnations]], a dying King Zakareth VI{{!}}King Zakareth is approached by the [[Ragnarus Incarnation]] with a dark offer of immortality.}}

== Chapter-by-chapter ==
* '''Chapter 1:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]], a powerful warrior guided by his advisor [[Korr]], intervenes in a violent encounter between two [[Enosh Travelers]] and a merchant family in the [[Latari Forest]]. After killing the [[Travelers]] to protect a young boy named [[Simon]], [[Indirial]] uses his abilities to navigate mental illusions and ensure the survivors reach safety. He vows to protect his territory from further threats as Midsummer approaches.}}
* '''Chapter 2:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|In the [[Badari Desert]], [[Kai]] uses his stone and [[steel]] powers to battle a pack of [[obsidian wolves]] before heading toward a sandstone temple. Meanwhile, in [[Valinhall]], [[Simon]] struggles to navigate the "[[rain garden]]" and encounters [[Erastes]], who expresses resentment toward him for the lives lost during [[Simon]]'s previous actions. The chapter concludes with [[Simon]] discovering that the [[Agnos family]] is repairing a powerful The mask{{!}}mask previously worn by [[Overlord Malachi]] and exploring a newly opened [[workshop]].}}
* '''Chapter 3:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] uses a scrying lens to watch her brother [[Talos]] and Lysander Torannus{{!}}Overlord Lysander observe an assassination attempt by their sister, [[Adessa]], against King Zakareth VI{{!}}King Zakareth. The coup fails when the King's bodyguard, [[Indirial]], swiftly neutralizes [[Adessa]]'s followers and the [[Heiress]] herself is exiled to [[Lirial]]. After [[Talos]] leaves, [[Leah]] realizes her brother is a dangerous threat and uses a recording crystal to study his movements for clues.}}
* '''Chapter 4:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Kai]] retrieves the legendary [[sword]] [[Mithra]] from a temple after defeating a golem, only to be confronted by the Eldest{{!}}Eldest Nye who leads him to the Ragnarus tree{{!}}Ragnarus Tree. At the tree, the [[Eldest]] uses [[Mithra]] to break the seal, releasing the long-imprisoned The Wanderer{{!}}Wanderer, [[Valin]], from his earthen prison. Following a brief and violent duel between the former mentor and student, [[Valin]] remains in the world while [[Kai]] escapes back to [[Valinhall]], leaving various leaders across the realm to react to Incarnation{{!}}the Incarnation's return.}}
* '''Chapter 5:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|While being fitted for golden armor by [[Master Farin]], [[Alin]] discusses the nature of [[Travelers]] and the dangers of [[Valinhall]] with [[Grandmaster Naraka]]. Meanwhile, [[Leah]], a [[Damascan]] heiress acting as a spy, arrives in [[Enosh]] via a [[Lirial Gate]] to monitor [[Alin]] and the [[Grandmasters]]. The chapter concludes with [[Alin]] reuniting with his three sisters before preparing to address the city in a celebration of his victory over [[Overlord Malachi]].}}
* '''Chapter 6:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|After returning to [[Enosh]], [[Simon]] is approached by [[Leah]], who questions him about his [[Valinhall]] training and displays an unsettling amount of knowledge regarding [[Travelers]]. [[Simon]] is later warned by a magical hummingbird that the [[Grandmasters]] do not trust him, yet he is still invited to attend their war council. During the meeting, [[Grandmaster Naraka]] reveals that an [[Incarnation]] has escaped its prison and proposes a preemptive strike against [[Damasca]] to capitalize on the chaos.}}
* '''Chapter 7:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|After a tense meeting with the [[Grandmasters]], [[Alin]] warns [[Simon]] that he is needed for an upcoming attack and provides him with a magical communication device. While [[Simon]] attempts to retreat into [[Valinhall]], he is ambushed in his room by three [[Gar'rosh]] assassins from [[Naraka]] seeking vengeance for his past killings. [[Kai]] arrives to rescue [[Simon]] and effortlessly dispatches the assassins, subsequently revealing that they must hunt down an escaped [[Valinhall Incarnation]].}}
* '''Chapter 8:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|After successfully spying on a meeting of the [[Enosh Grandmasters]] using a recording crystal, [[Leah]] discovers they are planning an attack and that the [[Valinhall Incarnation]] is marching toward her father's palace. She travels to [[Helgard]] to warn King Zakareth VI{{!}}King Zakareth, where she witnesses his powerful spear strike down a mirka monster. The chapter concludes with a tense political debate between [[Leah]], her father, and her brother [[Talos]] regarding whether to prioritize protecting individual villages or the stability of the realm's power structure.}}
* '''Chapter 9:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Simon]], [[Kai]], and [[Denner]] travel to a [[Damascan]] military camp where they meet [[Overlord Indirial]], who reveals that the [[Valinhall Incarnation]], [[Valin]], has escaped his prison and is heading toward the capital. As [[Indirial]] organizes a defense against both [[Valin]] and an impending attack by the [[Grandmasters]], [[Valin]] launches a sudden assault on the camp. Amidst the chaos of battle between soldiers and [[Nye]], [[Simon]] challenges [[Valin]] to a duel to protect [[Indirial]] and test his own strength.}}
* '''Chapter 10:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]], [[Grandmaster Naraka]], and [[Gilad]] embark on a mission to destroy the Overlords' [[Trees]] by infiltrating [[Malachi]]'s house, following news that an [[Incarnation]] has attacked the [[Damascans]]. During their journey through the frozen [[Territory]] of [[Helgard]], they are ambushed in a room by a mysterious Valinhall Travelers{{!}}Valinhall Traveler in black who easily defeats several soldiers. To bypass the combat and reach their objective, [[Alin]] takes charge and uses his powers to blast a hole through the floor to search for The Tree{{!}}the Tree below.}}
* '''Chapter 11:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|After Incarnation{{!}}the Incarnation [[Valin]] brutally defeats [[Indirial]] and begins a massacre of soldiers on his path toward [[Cana]], [[Simon]] drags the unconscious Overlord to safety. Simultaneously, [[Alin]] and his companions battle [[Endross Travelers]] before being ambushed by a white-haired [[Valinhall]] swordsman. [[Alin]] manages to retreat into his [[Territory]], [[Elysia]], with the injured [[Grandmaster Naraka]] and [[Gilad]], where the guide [[Rhalia]] intervenes to forcibly eject the intruder.}}
* '''Chapter 12:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|As Incarnation{{!}}the Incarnation's presence transforms the landscape into a wooden imitation of [[Valinhall]], [[Simon]] attempts to stall him but is interrupted by the arrival of [[Grandmasters]] and a war party from [[Enosh]]. King Zakareth VI{{!}}King Zakareth arrives with his army and [[Denner]], intervening in the conflict by hurling a powerful spear at [[Valin]] and forcing the [[Enosh Travelers]] to retreat through their [[Gates]]. The chapter concludes with [[Simon]] coming face-to-face with the King's daughter, [[Leah]], whom he recognizes from his past.}}
* '''Chapter 13:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|In [[Elysia]], [[Alin]] and [[Rhalia]] navigate the [[Gold District]] and reach a central chamber containing doors to nine different districts, where [[Rhalia]] explains that entering requires specific virtues to avoid becoming an '[[Incarnation]].' Meanwhile, [[Simon]] confronts [[Leah]] after discovering she is the second in line for the [[Damascan]] throne and had been undercover in [[Myria]] to find the [[Elysian Traveler]]. Feeling betrayed by her deception and her failure to prevent past tragedies, [[Simon]] rejects [[Leah]]'s explanations and uses a [[Valinhall Gate]] to return home.}}
* '''Chapter 14:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Simon]] successfully navigates the [[rain garden]]'s trials by leading the room rather than reacting to it, earning the Frozen Horn, an artifact used to close [[Gates]]. Meanwhile, [[Alin]] uses his new rose-colored healing power to stabilize the wounded [[Grandmaster Naraka]] before visiting his sisters to find a moment of normalcy amidst the aftermath of the failed attack.}}
* '''Chapter 15:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|In the [[workshop]], [[Simon]] tests a modified The mask{{!}}mask from Ragnarus the Crimson Vault{{!}}Ragnarus that grants him immense power but leaves his body covered in black, [[binding]] chains and completely draining his strength. [[Indirial]] reveals himself as an Overlord and confronts the Eldest{{!}}Eldest Nye about The mask{{!}}the mask's danger, ultimately deciding to keep it under watch rather than attuning it for use. Meanwhile, in the [[Crimson Vault]], King Zakareth VI{{!}}King Zakareth names his daughter [[Leah]] as his successor, tasking her with coordinating the defense of the realm against potential threats from [[Grandmasters]] and his son [[Talos]].}}
* '''Chapter 16:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Talos]], the [[Damascan Heir]], proposes an alliance to the [[Grandmasters]], suggesting he can open a [[Ragnarus Gate]] to allow them direct access to the royal palace in [[Cana]]. Despite initial distrust and a failed attempt by [[Talos]] to escape via an [[Avernus Gate]], the [[Grandmasters]] agree to work with him to strike at the [[Hanging Tree]]. [[Alin]] observes the tense negotiations, realizing that this desperate plan to attack [[Cana]] may be the [[Grandmasters]]' last hope for victory.}}
* '''Chapter 17:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|After traveling through the sweltering tunnels of [[Naraka]], [[Simon]] and his companions arrive at the massive [[Damascan army]] camp near [[Enosh]] to prepare for an upcoming assault. [[Simon]] meets with [[Leah]], who reveals she has been named the royal [[Successor]] and warns him to watch out for her brother, [[Talos]], who is resentful of her position. [[Simon]] and [[Denner]] are placed in reserve under [[Talos]]'s command as the army prepares to face the city and its potential [[Incarnations]].}}
* '''Chapter 18:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|After [[Simon]] finds [[Kai]] whittling and learns about the history of his duel with [[Indirial]], [[Talos]] challenges [[Simon]] to a sparring match that is interrupted by an ambush from [[Avernus Travelers]]. Following a chaotic battle involving psychic attacks and bird-men, [[Denner]] uses a glass bell to banish the enemy forces, but the victory is short-lived. The chapter concludes as a mysterious man with black tattoos and a silver-gold [[sword]] emerges from a Gate, revealing himself to be [[Valin]].}}
* '''Chapter 19:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|In the Naraka Quarters{{!}}Naraka quarters, [[Gilad]] performs a painful ritual to restore [[Grandmaster Naraka]]'s [[Traveler]] status by branding her palm with a white-hot symbol. Meanwhile, an ambush erupts as the [[Valinhall Incarnation]] and various [[Travelers]] clash with [[Damascan]] forces amidst a chaotic battlefield featuring a massive winged serpent. During the fray, Talos{{!}}Heir Talos betrays [[Kai]] with a fatal strike, prompting [[Simon]] to intervene and attempt to find a way to save him.}}
* '''Chapter 20:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] discovers Lysander Torannus{{!}}Overlord Lysander's attempt to assassinate her by falsely accusing [[Simon]] of betrayal, leading her to trap the traitor in a crystal coffin. Meanwhile, [[Simon]] obtains a powerful The mask{{!}}mask from the Eldest{{!}}Eldest Nye to combat the [[Valinhall Incarnation]] and joins forces with [[Andra]] and [[Erastes]]. The chapter concludes with [[Simon]] using The mask{{!}}the mask's immense power to engage Incarnation{{!}}the Incarnation in a high-speed battle, successfully knocking the entity back with a heavy blow.}}
* '''Chapter 21:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] and a group of [[Travelers]] pass through a Gate to reach the [[Damascan camp]], where they encounter the [[Valinhall Incarnation]] and a masked [[Simon]] engaged in a violent battle. As [[Simon]] struggles against the physical toll of his power, [[Leah]] uses her crown to bind and subdue Incarnation{{!}}the Incarnation, allowing [[Simon]] to deliver a fatal blow with [[Talos's sword]]. Following the battle, an exhausted [[Simon]] is taken for medical care while [[Alin]] arrives on the scene.}}
* '''Chapter 22:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] follows the [[Grandmasters]] to find a wounded Talos{{!}}Heir Talos, whom he heals before the group enters the [[Crimson Vault]]. After witnessing [[Simon]]'s victory over Incarnation{{!}}the Incarnation and discovering that his friend [[Leah]] is actually [[Damascan]] royalty, a betrayed [[Alin]] enters the Vault to join the battle. Inside, he faces King Zakareth VI{{!}}King Zakareth, who dominates the [[Enosh Travelers]] until [[Grandmaster Naraka]] summons the [[Arbiter]] of Betrayal to challenge the King.}}
* '''Chapter 23:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|Following the death of King Zakareth VI{{!}}King Zakareth, [[Alin]] secures a [[Hanging Tree seed]] and escapes [[Cana]] just as the [[Ragnarus Incarnation]] is unleashed. Meanwhile, [[Grandmaster Naraka]] confronts the traitorous [[Talos]] and later travels to the burning city of [[Bel Calem]] to meet the [[Naraka Incarnation]]. She intends to use the information regarding the seed to further her own agenda and influence the unfolding chaos.}}
* '''Chapter 24:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|After returning from [[Myria]], [[Alin]] is confronted by [[Grandmaster Naraka]], who attempts to coerce him into surrendering the seed of the [[Hanging Tree]] by threatening his sisters with a [[Naraka Incarnation]]. [[Alin]] rushes back to [[Myria]] only to find the village in flames and his sisters under attack, leading to a massive battle where he summons the armies of [[Elysia]]. Ultimately, [[Alin]] achieves a state of immense power and kills Incarnation{{!}}the Incarnation, though he discovers that [[Shai]] is dead and [[Tamara]] has also perished.}}
* '''Chapter 25:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|After waking in [[Abdera]], [[Simon]] encounters a transformed [[Alin]], who has become an [[Incarnation]] and tasks [[Simon]] with protecting his sister, [[Ilana]]. [[Simon]] brings the unconscious [[Ilana]] to the [[House of Blades]], where he joins [[Leah]], [[Indirial]], and [[Kai]] to plan a resistance against the roaming [[Incarnations]]. Meanwhile, in the [[Crimson Vault]], the dying King Zakareth VI{{!}}King Zakareth is approached by the [[Ragnarus Incarnation]], who offers him immortal power in exchange for an unspecified price.}}

== Characters ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Simon]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Kai]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valin]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Talos]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Grandmaster Naraka]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[King Zakareth VI]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Denner]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Lysander]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valinhall Incarnation]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Caela]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Gilad]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Otoku]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Eliadel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Angeline]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Grandmaster Endross]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Zakareth]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Eldest]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Grandmaster Avernus]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Malachi]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[The Wanderer]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Adessa]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Tamara]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[King Zakareth the Sixth]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Overlord Malachi]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Chaka]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Olissa]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Ilana]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Rhalia]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Elysian Traveler]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Kathrin]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Lysander Torannus]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Grandmaster Lirial]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Haresh]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Cormac]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Lilia]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Benson]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Erastes]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Zakareth the Sixth]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Grandmaster Asphodel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Grandmaster Helgard]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Lizard-man]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Seliethin]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Overlord (The Traveler's Gate Trilogy)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|man in black}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Nye Eldest]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Andra]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Adrienne]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Deborah]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Korr]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Asphodel Traveler]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Olissa Agnos]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Cynara]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Shai]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[King (The Traveler's Gate Trilogy)]]}}

== Locations & groups ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valinhall]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Enosh]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Damasca]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valinhall Travelers]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Cana]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Naraka]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Nye]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Myria]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Crimson Vault]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Elysia]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Dragon Army]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Grandmasters]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Tartarus Travelers]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Tartarus]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Enosh Travelers]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Endross]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Helgard]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Avernus Travelers]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Latari Forest]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[House]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Ornheim]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Avernus]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Harinfel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Travelers]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Bel Calem]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Damascan soldiers]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Enosh Grandmasters]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Endross Travelers]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Damascan army]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Sarin tribe sparrows]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Lirial Travelers]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Grandmasters’ palace]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Silver District]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Overlords (The Traveler's Gate Trilogy)]]}}

== Abilities & sacred arts ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Ragnarus the Crimson Vault]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Hanging Tree]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Lirial]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[steel]]}}

== Artifacts & constructs ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Dragon's Fang]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Mithra]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Azura (The Traveler's Gate Trilogy)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[The mask]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[frozen horn]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Benson's steel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Vasha]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Ragnarus Gate]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Hanging Tree seed]]}}

== Events & concepts ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Naraka Incarnation]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Incarnation]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Naraka Traveler]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Nye essence]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valinhall Gate]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Territory]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[gate (The Traveler's Gate Trilogy)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Incarnations]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Asphodel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Tartarus steel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Ragnarus Traveler]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Tree]]}}

== Sources ==
* https://openlibrary.org/works/OL22028959W

{{Willverse books nav}}

[[Category:Books]]
[[Category:The Crimson Vault]]
